metric_name: Root cause of FTI receivables expansion
metric_value: Symptom of poor operational cost control (Cost Push mode), not credit deterioration
notes: Key analytical conclusion of EVP 5; receivables are distorted by hockey-stick shipments

metric_value: Heavy weighting of final two weeks of quarterly shipments artificially inflates quarter-end DSO
notes: Actual average monthly receivables considerably less than quarter-end statements show

metric_name: FY82 plan: no change in terms, delinquencies, or returns
metric_value: Receivables quality unchanged; stated increase is presentation artifact
notes: Three-point conclusion: no change in terms, monthly avg < quarter-end, distortion from shipment weighting

metric_name: Institutional investor focus on balance sheets
metric_value: Balance sheets neglected by Wall Street and company management; P/E focus dominates
notes: CFO is 'in the minority' at FTI; BAM analyst unusual in focusing on assets

metric_name: Inventory valuation vs receivables valuation asymmetry
metric_value: Inventory = 40 cents/dollar (cost); Receivables = $1.00/dollar (revenue); three inventory categories vs one receivables category; 10-Q required for detail
notes: Casale argues this asymmetry means a dollar of receivables != a dollar of inventories analytically

metric_value: Numbers are useful but not to the degree of precision that some wish; comparison of original vs restated figures reveals limitations
notes: EVP 5 closing philosophical point on accounting precision in fast-growth companies

metric_name: EVP series scope and publication frequency
metric_value: EVP 1-5 focused on hypergrowth phenomenon; series is aperiodic, issued in response to current investor interest topics
notes: EVP 6 and beyond to address broader industry and market topics
